title: Meta releases first new AI model since shaking up team

title: Latest Anthropic AI model finds cracks in software defenses
description: Anthropic says its yet-to-be-released artificial intelligence model called Claude Mythos has proven keenly adept at exposing software weaknesses. Mythos has laid bare thousands of vulnerabilities in commonly used applications for which no patch or fix exists, prompting the San Francisco-based AI startup to form an alliance with cybersecurity specialists to…

title: NASCAR Appoints First AI Director as Richard Bowman Steps Into Leadership Role
description: NASCAR names Richard Bowman as its first AI Director, signaling a major step toward using artificial intelligence across racing operations

title: Ex-Pa. state trooper admits to child porn charges, illegal use of work computer
description: A disgraced former Pennsylvania State Police trooper admitted to multiple crimes, including possessing child sexual abuse material and lewd images and videos he created using artificial intelligence on his work computer at the state police barracks in Skippack Township.

title: Banks Selling $3 Billion Debt for Meta’s Prometheus Data Center
description: A group of banks including Natixis SA, Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group Inc. and Societe Generale SA have started selling $3 billion of loans for a data center in Ohio backed by Meta Platforms Inc., the latest deal supporting the buildout of artificial intelligence infrastructure.

title: Goldman Sachs uncovers a troubling pattern behind AI, tech job losses
description: Workers displaced by artificial intelligence and other tech take longer to find new jobs -- and when they do, they’re stuck earning less for years, a new study found.
content:
Workers displaced by artificial intelligence and other tech take longer to find new jobs — and when they do, they’re stuck earning less for years, a new study found.

title: Texas Data Center Tax Break Faces Rollback
description: The exemption, which applies to equipment, infrastructure, and electricity used by data centers, has expanded sharply with the growth of artificial intelligence and large-scale computing demand.
content:
Texas lawmakers are considering whether to scale back or eliminate a rapidly growing tax break for data centers.
The costs to the state are projected at $3.2 billion in lost sales tax revenue over the next two years, the Texas Tribune reported.
